location.office_time_required: full-time onsite at San Francisco HQ

level_signals: at least 1-2 years of relevant experience
eager to grow within a role that challenges and develops your skills
role involves hands-on operational execution and calibration tasks

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: you have at least 1-2 years of relevant experience and are eager to grow within a role that challenges and develops your skills.
Work closely with external vendors, trainers and internal researchers to collect, review, and deliver high-quality data
Gather requirements, write instructions, define success criteria, and calibrate the AI trainers
Use internal tooling to assess labeled data and provide feedback to AI trainers
Think critically and share recommendations on tooling and process improvements, optimizing for quality, throughput, and AI trainer experience
You've gone beyond basic data annotation and have hands-on experience with data analytics, using insights to inform decisions and improve processes.
You are comfortable navigating ambiguity, managing shifting priorities, and adapting to fast-paced changes without missing a beat.
You're an excellent communicator. You love bringing people together, fostering collaboration, and providing clarity in complex situations.
Even when things are moving quickly, you keep track of details, manage competing demands, and bring structure to complexity.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You're curious about AI, LLMs, Agents. While not required, an interest or background in these areas will help you connect the dots in our broader mission.
If you enjoy the hustle, resourcefulness, and rapid iteration typical of start-ups, you'll feel right at home.
While you might have an interest in broader topics like safety, you find satisfaction in executing operational tasks efficiently and effectively.

scope.surface: Human Data team — end-to-end data collection programs for training and evaluation of AI models
scope.business_model: internal-facing, supporting AI research and model training at OpenAI
core_responsibilities: Execute complex programs for collecting high-quality human data
Interface between external vendors and AI trainers
Gather requirements, write instructions, define success criteria, and calibrate AI trainers
Use internal tooling to assess labeled data and provide feedback to AI trainers
Recommend tooling and process improvements optimizing for quality, throughput, and AI trainer experience

green_flags: Clear product surface and team remit described in detail
Role has direct impact on model training at OpenAI
Low years-of-experience bar (1-2 years) — accessible to earlier-career candidates
JD explicitly encourages curiosity and growth mindset rather than rigid credential requirements
red_flags: Compensation not disclosed anywhere in the JD
Role is fully onsite at San Francisco HQ — no remote or hybrid option stated
JD is light on specifics around tooling and day-to-day workflow, making it hard to assess fit precisely

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 1-2 years experience signal and operational scope of role)
scope.business_model (inferred from team description — internal research support)
location.office_time_required (inferred from 'This role is based in our San Francisco HQ' — no hybrid language used)
ambiguities: The JD mentions 'at least 1-2 years of relevant experience' under a 'You'll thrive if' section rather than a hard 'Required' section, making severity ambiguous. Treated as hard given the unconditional framing and role context.

Data analytics hands-on experience is described as a positive differentiator ('You have a knack for data') but framed in the same 'You'll thrive if' section — could be soft; treated as hard given it is described as going 'beyond basic data annotation'.
